title: whittieria engelmannii
intro: Whittieria engelmannii, also known as the Engelmann's tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lauraceae. It is native to Central America and southern Mexico, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Whittieria engelmannii include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
